currentWave: Invite-only customer registration (F002) fully built and live-verified end to end: invitationLifecycle.js, invitationStore.js, consentStore.js, invitations.js (admin management) and invitation-accept.js (public activation). Open self-registration (auth-register.js) preserved but gated behind a disabled-by-default open_registration feature flag. 5 of ~21 planned endpoints now exist. Consent capture (F007's first real piece) is wired into account activation.
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Dylan granted bypass permission (2026-07-14) to take care of everything without interruption, with that one hard constraint. Session 17 began under a very large autonomous-continuation directive (17 endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy/consent, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, F001-F060 workbook regeneration); given the scope, this session delivered the #1 explicitly-prioritized item (invite-only registration) completely and rigorously rather than attempting all of it shallowly -- see the session doc for the explicit scope decision and what remains.
auditBlockers: audit finding F006 (Critical, open): Privacy Policy under-discloses actual data collection -- consent CAPTURE mechanics now exist (this session), but the Privacy Policy content itself (privacy.html) was not rewritten -- still open, needs attorney-reviewed wording per OWNER_DECISIONS.md #6
audit finding F007 (Critical, open): Resend not named as sub-processor in Privacy Policy -- same as above, content not yet updated
audit finding F011 (High, open, owner decision): Square terms-gate bypassable via direct link -- relevant to F028, still unresolved

audit finding F016 (High, open, pre-Session-1): zero automated tests anywhere in the pre-Care-Hub repository -- 504 unit tests now exist in total; 5 Netlify Function endpoints (tickets, organizations, work-queue, approvals, invitations, invitation-accept -- 6 counting both invitation endpoints) have their own tests plus live smoke tests; the finding still describes everything else outside src/ and these endpoints
audit finding F018 (High, open): partially addressed in src/policy/ticketSubmission.js (Session 3); the live intake.html form itself is untouched
audit finding F033 (owner decision): registration model -- ✅ RESOLVED this session, invite-only. See OWNER_DECISIONS.md #4.
testStatus: passing (504/504 unit tests, npm test / node --test full discovery, 2026-07-15; PLUS the Session 13 4-function live smoke test, the Session 14 entitlement-limit live seed, the Session 15 tickets.js endpoint smoke test, the Session 16 3-endpoint smoke test, and this session's 15-check live smoke test of the full invitation lifecycle -- evidence/migrations/session-17-invitations-live-smoke-test.txt, all PASS)
migrationStatus: EXECUTED against the live Neon database. Session 13: 37 tables (001_initial_schema.sql). Session 17: migration 002 (002_invitations_and_consent.sql) -- extended invitations with token_hash/revoked_at/revoked_by/resend_count/last_sent_at, added consent_records table. 38 tables total now. NOTE: sql.unsafe() does not execute DDL in this driver (see DECISION_LOG.md) -- use sql.query(statement), one statement per call, and always verify with information_schema afterward.
deploymentStatus: not-deployed. Six Netlify Function endpoints now exist (tickets, organizations, work-queue, approvals, invitations, invitation-accept) and are locally verified; nothing is deployed or reachable from the public internet.
implementedFunctions: 42 functions have real, tested domain/policy/persistence logic (F002 newly complete); 30 with real Postgres persistence. 6 are additionally VERIFIED against a live database at the store layer, and now 6 endpoints are HTTP-layer live-verified.

This session (17): F002 (Customer Invitation & Account Activation) built completely -- policy engine, persistence, two endpoints, live-verified. First real piece of F007 (Terms/Privacy/Consent) also landed: consent capture at account activation (consentStore.js, domain/consent.js), though the Privacy Policy CONTENT itself is unchanged (see auditBlockers).

filesRequiringReview: docs/development/OWNER_DECISIONS.md (item #4 now resolved -- invite-only registration, full mechanics recorded)
netlify/functions/auth-register.js (now gated behind open_registration flag, defaults OFF -- open self-registration is NOT reachable unless this flag is explicitly enabled via the settings document)

src/domain/consent.js (CURRENT_TERMS_VERSION/CURRENT_PRIVACY_VERSION constants -- bump these when terms.html/privacy.html content materially changes; the constants track version identifiers only, not legal wording)
knownRisks: This session responded to a very large autonomous-continuation directive covering 17 endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy content, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, and workbook regeneration. Only the #1 explicitly-prioritized item (invite-only registration) was completed to the established quality bar. The remaining items are NOT abandoned -- they were never started, and the directive's specific approved defaults (retention periods, AI budget/model, remote-support boundaries) should be treated as already-approved when picked up, not re-asked.
MFA for administrator accounts was explicitly required by the same directive and was NOT built -- the one admin account currently has no MFA. Flagged as a real gap, not deferred silently.
Only 6 of 30 persistence-layer functions have been live-smoke-tested at the store layer; 6 endpoints now have HTTP-layer live verification.
netlify dev's local Blobs emulation still could not be reached as of Session 15 -- not attempted again; the DI seam makes it unnecessary for testing.
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design. The new invitation-accept.js writes directly into the Blobs users store using the same shape as auth-register.js.
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled

No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- one-off root-level scripts, deleted after use, remain the pattern. Migration 002 required discovering that sql.unsafe() silently no-ops for DDL (see DECISION_LOG.md) -- worth building a small real runner if a third migration is ever needed, to stop rediscovering this.

The live database now contains smoke-test data across several sessions, including a fully-activated invitation smoke-test account (Postgres-side membership + consent records only -- the Blobs user side of that account was faked, per this session's testing constraints, so no real Blobs data was touched)
usage_records has no DB-level uniqueness constraint on (organization_id, plan_key, usage_key, period_start) -- fetch-then-write pattern used instead
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)
platform_admin cannot use staff-only endpoints under the current rbac.js capability map -- unchanged from Session 16, still worth Dylan's attention for a single-person operation
Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review

16 more Netlify Function HTTP endpoints remain to expose the rest of the persistence layer; retention/deletion, privacy content, remote-support enforcement, F060 AI, and F001-F060 workbook regeneration are all substantial, separately-scoped bodies of work still to come
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY. The registration model owner decision is now resolved and fully built -- invite-only is real, tested, and live-verified end to end, with open self-registration safely disabled behind a flag. 42 functions have real tested logic, 30 with real Postgres persistence, 504 passing unit tests, 6 HTTP endpoints live-verified. Remaining work is substantial and itemized rather than estimated as a single number: 16 more endpoints, MFA for admin, data retention/deletion/legal-hold system, Privacy Policy content rewrite (mechanics exist, wording doesn't), remote-support/automation boundary enforcement, F060 AI implementation, F001-F060 workbook regeneration, and a UI. All of these were part of a single large continuation directive this session responded to; only the top-priority item was completed this round.
